title: Help and support - Lilian Greenwood MP
text_excerpt: Help and support - Lilian Greenwood MP Skip to main content Home About Lilian Contact Lilian Apply for PMQs tickets Information for schools Christmas Card Competition 2025 Office Standards Policy Help and support Privacy Notice More Menu Help and support If you are a constituent in Nottingham South, please do contact Lilian at any point if you think she can assist you. Lilian is here to work and represent you and has supported thousands of constituents since she was first elected in 2010. Urgent Assistance In an emergency, please call 999. NHS – 111 Nottinghamshire Police – 101 Nottingham City Council – 0115 915 5555 Nottingham Housing Aid – 0115 876 3300 or housingaid@nottinghamcity.gov.uk Meadows Advice Group – 0115 986 0197 Clifton Advice Centre – 0115 940 5551 Juno Women’s Aid (Domestic Abuse) – 0808 800 0340 Wellness in Mind Nottingham – 0800 561 0073 Childline – 0800 1111 Housing and homelessness Shelter – 0344 515 2000 Nottingham Housing Aid – 0115 876 3300 or housingaid@nottinghamcity.gov.uk Street Support Nottingham – nottingham@streetsupport.net Framework Housing Association – 0115 841 7711 Food banks Please note that this is not an extensive list of food banks in Nottingham. Please visit the Trussell Trust website if you do not see your area below. The Meadows – 0303 040 1110 – Bridgeway Centre, Arkwright Street, The Meadows, Nottingham, NG2 2JD. Clifton – 07538 916 757 – Hope Centre, Southchurch Drive, Clifton, Nottingham, NG11 8AR. Wollaton – 0303 040 1110 – Grangewood Methodist Church, Grangewood Road, Nottingham, NG8 2SJ. Bilborough – 0303 040 1110 – Bilborough Library, Bracebridge Drive, Bilborough, Nottingham, NG8 4PN. Nottingham City Council For help with bins, council tax, adult social care or licences, please use the Nottingham City Council website or call 0115 915 5555. Councillors are elected to the local council to represent a ward and local community. Find your ward and local councillors by clicking here . Legal advice Nottingham Law Centre – 0115 978 7813 – 119 Radford Road, Hyson Green, Nottingham, NG7 5DU. NLS Legal (based at Nottingham Law School) is for those unable to afford legal services and who are unable to access legal aid. You can make an enquiry here . Citizens Advice Nottingham – 0808 278 7974 – 16-18 Maid Marian Way, Nottingham NG1 6HS, United Kingdom. Children and young people If you are concerned about a child in Nottingham you can contact the city’s Multi Agency Safeguarding (Children) Hub (City MASH) at 0115 876 4800 or CityMASH@nottinghamcity.gov.uk. The Hub is open Monday to Friday from 8:30 am to 4:50 pm. Outside of these hours, the telephone number should only be used for emergencies. Base 51 – supports 11-25 year olds – 0115 952 5040 or info@base51.org.uk. Nottingham City Council Youth Service provides information on youth provision, children’s activity centres, activities for children and families, and holiday activities. Support for pensioners I would encourage everyone to check what they are eligible for the following resources: Check if you are eligible for Pension Credit online at www.gov.uk/pension-credit/eligibility or call 0800 99 1234 . ⁠Check if you are eligible for Attendance Allowance, a disability benefit for those over retirement age, which is not means tested, at www.gov.uk/attendance-allowance or call 0800 731 0122 . Contact Nottingham City Council to discuss Housing Benefit and Council Tax Reduction. Check can find further information here: https://www.nottinghamcity.gov.uk/information-for-residents/benefits/ . ⁠Contact your local Citizen’s Advice on 0808 802 2000 . Turn2Us – The charity Turn2Us can help people fill out forms and check eligibility. Visit https://www.turn2us.org.uk or call 0808 802 2000. ⁠Use the Turn2Us online benefits calculator: benefits-calculator.turn2us.org.uk/. Check if you are eligible for any grants with Turn2Us: grants-search.turn2us.org.uk/ . Dementia Support Nottinghamshire Carers Association – 01773 833833. Other Dementia support and
